evals.name: Install Argo CD on OpenShift via GitOps Operator
evals.prompt: Install Argo CD on my OpenShift cluster using the GitOps operator. I want the Route enabled with reencrypt TLS, OpenShift OAuth via Dex, RBAC that maps 'cluster-admins' group to admin role with default readonly policy, resource exclusions for Tekton TaskRun/PipelineRun, and notifications enabled.
expectations: Generates an openshift-gitops-operator Subscription resource for the redhat-operators CatalogSource in the openshift-operators namespace
Generates an ArgoCD CR with apiVersion argoproj.io/v1beta1 in the openshift-gitops namespace
ArgoCD CR has spec.server.route.enabled: true with tls.termination: reencrypt
ArgoCD CR has dex.openShiftOAuth: true for OAuth integration
ArgoCD CR has rbac.defaultPolicy: role:readonly and policy mapping cluster-admins to role:admin
ArgoCD CR has resourceExclusions for tekton.dev TaskRun and PipelineRun
ArgoCD CR has notifications.enabled: true
Shows a dry-run or preview of what will be created before applying
Explicitly asks for user confirmation before applying any resource
Includes a verification step to check pods are running after apply

evals.name: Create Application for OCI Helm chart with automated sync
evals.prompt: Create an Application for my Helm chart at oci://registry.example.com/myapp version 2.x in the production namespace with automated sync, selfHeal, and prune enabled. The project is 'platform' and the cluster is in-cluster. Set CreateNamespace and ServerSideApply sync options.
expectations: Generates an Application with apiVersion argoproj.io/v1alpha1 in the argocd namespace
Source uses repoURL: oci://registry.example.com with chart field for the OCI chart
targetRevision uses a semver constraint matching 2.x (e.g., '2.x', '>=2.0.0 <3.0.0', or '2.*')
Destination namespace is production with server https://kubernetes.default.svc
spec.project is set to platform
syncPolicy has automated with prune: true and selfHeal: true
syncOptions includes CreateNamespace=true and ServerSideApply=true
Shows a dry-run preview (kubectl apply --dry-run=server or --dry-run=client) of the Application
Explicitly asks for user confirmation before applying
Includes post-apply verification checking sync status

evals.name: Promote canary rollout in production
evals.prompt: Promote the canary rollout frontend in the production namespace.
expectations: Checks the current rollout status BEFORE promoting (kubectl argo rollouts get/status or kubectl get rollout)
Shows the current phase, step index, and what step the rollout is on
Explains what promoting will do (advance to the next step or increase canary weight)
Shows the promote command that will be executed
Explicitly asks for user confirmation before promoting
Executes kubectl argo rollouts promote frontend -n production after confirmation
Includes a monitoring/verification step after promotion
Does NOT use --full unless the user explicitly requests full promotion

evals.name: Upgrade Argo CD from 2.12 to 2.14
evals.prompt: Upgrade Argo CD from 2.12 to 2.14 on my cluster. It was installed with Helm.
expectations: Checks the current Argo CD version first (argocd version or image inspection)
Mentions checking release notes for breaking changes between 2.12 and 2.14
Recommends backing up Applications and AppProjects before upgrading
Generates CRD update commands (kubectl apply --server-side for application/appproject/applicationset CRDs)
Applies CRDs before the Helm upgrade (correct ordering)
Generates helm upgrade command with the target version and --dry-run for preview
Explicitly asks for user confirmation before applying the upgrade
Includes post-upgrade verification (rollout status, argocd version)
Does NOT skip CRD updates (Helm does not update CRDs on upgrade)

evals.name: Backup all Argo CD resources
evals.prompt: Back up all my Argo CD Applications and AppProjects to YAML files.
expectations: Exports Applications using kubectl get applications -n argocd -o yaml or iterates individually
Exports AppProjects using kubectl get appprojects -n argocd -o yaml or iterates individually
Saves each resource type to separate files or a structured backup directory
Reports the count of exported resources for both Applications and AppProjects
Also exports or offers to export ConfigMaps (argocd-cm, argocd-rbac-cm)
Also exports or offers to export credential Secrets (repo and cluster) with a security warning
Warns that Secret backup files contain sensitive data and should be encrypted
Does NOT modify or delete any resources (read-only backup operation)
Does NOT require user confirmation for read-only export (but may confirm the backup directory)
